glossary.plain_meaning: TouchDesigner is software for moving information around so it turns into images, sound, or interaction while it is happening.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not software where you work on one finished file and then press play.
glossary.mental_model: A system where things are always moving instead of being worked on one at a time.
glossary.example: Imagine a video of a train moving forward on tracks. TouchDesigner is the whole rail system that keeps the train moving.

glossary.plain_meaning: Signal flow means information moves in a direction, step by step, from one place to the next.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not information jumping randomly or happening all at once.
glossary.example: The train moves forward along the tracks. Each section of track is part of the signal flow.

glossary.plain_meaning: An operator is one small part of the system that does one job and then passes the result along.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not the whole project by itself.
glossary.mental_model: One station the train passes through.
glossary.example: The train passes through a station where something changes, like picking up passengers, then continues moving.

glossary.plain_meaning: OP taxonomy is how TouchDesigner groups operators by what kind of job they do.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not about which operators are better or worse.
glossary.mental_model: Labels that say what each station is meant for.
glossary.example: Different stations exist for different purposes, even though the same train passes through all of them.

glossary.plain_meaning: Real time means things update immediately while they are happening.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not something that waits until the end to show a result.
glossary.example: You are watching a train move on the tracks as it moves, not watching a video later.

glossary.plain_meaning: A network is a group of connected parts that pass information to each other.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not a single part working by itself.
glossary.example: The train moves through many connected tracks instead of just one short track.

glossary.plain_meaning: TOPs work with images.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These do not work with numbers or text.
glossary.example: The actual video of the train moving down the tracks.

glossary.plain_meaning: CHOPs work with changing numbers.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not images.
glossary.mental_model: Values that move up and down.
glossary.example: The train’s speed going faster or slower over time.

glossary.plain_meaning: SOPs work with shapes in space.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not flat pictures.
glossary.example: The shape of the train and the rails it sits on.

glossary.plain_meaning: DATs work with text and lists.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These do not make images.
glossary.example: A written list of train stops and times.

glossary.plain_meaning: MATs control how surfaces look.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These do not change shape or position.
glossary.example: The color and finish of the train’s outer shell.

glossary.plain_meaning: Input is information that comes into something.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not the final result.
glossary.example: The train entering the tracks at the start.

glossary.plain_meaning: Output is information that comes out after something has happened to it.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not where information starts.
glossary.example: The train leaving the tracks at the end.

glossary.plain_meaning: A viewer is a place where you can see what is happening.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change what is happening.
glossary.example: Watching the train through a window as it passes by.

glossary.plain_meaning: A parameter is a setting that changes how something behaves.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not the thing itself.
glossary.example: Changing how fast the train moves by adjusting a speed control.

glossary.plain_meaning: Cook means the system is doing its work right now.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not saving or rendering something for later.
glossary.example: The train is moving on the tracks right now, not parked.

glossary.plain_meaning: Time is what keeps everything moving forward.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not a timeline you scrub back and forth.
glossary.example: The train keeps moving forward and does not stop unless something makes it stop.

glossary.plain_meaning: Data is the information itself. View is how you look at that information.
glossary.what_it_is_not: Seeing something does not mean it is changed.
glossary.mental_model: The thing versus the window showing the thing.
glossary.example: The train is real data. Watching the train through a window is the view.

glossary.plain_meaning: State means something remembers what happened before.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not something that resets every moment.
glossary.example: The train remembers how fast it was going a moment ago instead of starting over.

glossary.plain_meaning: A frame is one update of the system.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not a long stretch of time.
glossary.example: Each small movement you see as the train moves forward is one frame.

glossary.plain_meaning: Rate is how many frames happen over time.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not how big or strong something is.
glossary.example: How fast the train appears to move because many frames happen quickly.

glossary.plain_meaning: Context means where something exists and what it belongs to.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not the thing itself.
glossary.example: The same train looks different depending on whether you see it from the station, a bridge, or inside another train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Inside means you are looking at something from within it. Outside means you are looking at it from the outside.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not about distance or zoom.
glossary.mental_model: Being in a room versus standing outside the room.
glossary.example: Being inside the train car versus watching the train pass from the platform.

glossary.plain_meaning: Live means something keeps changing. Stored means something stays the same.
glossary.what_it_is_not: Stored does not mean deleted or gone.
glossary.example: A train currently moving is live. A photo of the train is stored.

glossary.plain_meaning: A trigger or event is something that happens once when a condition is met.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not something that happens every frame.
glossary.example: The moment the train horn sounds as it enters the station.

glossary.plain_meaning: A channel is one stream of changing numbers.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not an image or a block of text.
glossary.example: One channel could be the train’s speed changing over time.

glossary.plain_meaning: Units tell you what a number means.
glossary.what_it_is_not: A number without units does not explain itself.
glossary.example: Knowing whether the train moved 10 meters, 10 seconds, or 10 frames.

glossary.plain_meaning: The palette is a place where ready-made parts are listed so you can bring them into your work.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not run anything by itself.
glossary.mental_model: A shelf of parts you can pick from.
glossary.example: A yard next to the tracks where train parts are stored before being used.

glossary.plain_meaning: Derivative is the main group of built-in parts made by the creators of the software.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not your personal collection.
glossary.example: Standard train cars made by the train company.

glossary.plain_meaning: Generators are parts that create something from nothing.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These do not need input to start.
glossary.example: A train starting its journey at the first station.

glossary.plain_meaning: Image filters change the look of images.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These do not create new images from scratch.
glossary.example: Changing how the train video looks, like darker or blurrier.

glossary.plain_meaning: Mapping is about fitting images onto shapes or spaces.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change the image content itself.
glossary.example: Projecting the train video onto the side of a building.

glossary.plain_meaning: Meta Quest groups parts made to work with VR headsets.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not general image or sound work.
glossary.example: Viewing the train scene through a VR headset.

glossary.plain_meaning: POPs are parts that work with many small moving points.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not single solid objects.
glossary.mental_model: Lots of tiny pieces moving together.
glossary.example: Seeing sparks or dust around the train as many small moving dots.

glossary.plain_meaning: TD Ableton groups parts that connect TouchDesigner to Ableton.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not play music by itself.
glossary.example: The train syncing its movement to music coming from another control room.

glossary.plain_meaning: TD Bitwing groups parts that connect TouchDesigner to Bitwing.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not general sound control.
glossary.example: The train receiving timing signals from a different control system.

glossary.plain_meaning: TD Synchro groups parts used to keep things in time together.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not create content.
glossary.example: Making sure multiple trains arrive at the station at the same time.

glossary.plain_meaning: TDVR groups parts made for virtual reality setups.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not for flat screens.
glossary.example: Standing inside the train scene instead of watching it on a screen.

glossary.plain_meaning: TDVS groups parts made for video server workflows.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not simple playback.
glossary.example: Sending the train video to many screens at once.

glossary.plain_meaning: Techniques groups parts that help manage how the system runs.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These do not create visuals directly.
glossary.example: Systems that keep the train schedule running smoothly.

glossary.plain_meaning: Thread Manager helps control how work is split up inside the system.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change what the work is.
glossary.example: Deciding which tracks trains use so they don’t collide.

glossary.plain_meaning: Tools are helpers that make work easier.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not the main content.
glossary.example: Tools used by workers to inspect the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: UI groups parts used to build interfaces people interact with.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not visuals meant only to be watched.
glossary.example: Buttons that start or stop the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Vive groups parts made to work with Vive hardware.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not general VR support.
glossary.example: Controlling the train scene using Vive controllers.

glossary.plain_meaning: Web RTC groups parts for sending data over the internet in real time.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not saved file sharing.
glossary.example: Watching the train live from another city.

glossary.plain_meaning: My Components is your personal collection of saved parts.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not built-in defaults.
glossary.example: Train parts you built yourself and keep for later.

glossary.plain_meaning: This area shows details and hints about what is selected.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change anything.
glossary.example: A sign next to the tracks that explains what this station does.

glossary.plain_meaning: Live 11 holds parts made to work with Ableton Live version 11.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These parts are not for other versions of Ableton.
glossary.mental_model: Parts made for one specific train model.
glossary.example: Tracks designed only for a newer train so it runs correctly.

glossary.plain_meaning: Live 9+10 holds parts made to work with older versions of Ableton Live.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These parts are not for Live 11.
glossary.mental_model: Parts made for older train models.
glossary.example: Tracks built for older trains so they still run safely.

glossary.plain_meaning: Extras holds additional parts that are not required but can be useful.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not the main connection parts.
glossary.example: Extra train cars you can attach if you want.

glossary.plain_meaning: System Components are parts that help the overall system work together.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not visuals or content by themselves.
glossary.example: The engines and controls that keep the train running.

glossary.plain_meaning: Utilities are small helper parts that do simple support jobs.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not main features.
glossary.example: Tools used to check the train before it moves.

glossary.plain_meaning: World Objects are parts that exist inside a 3D space.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not flat images.
glossary.example: The train, tracks, and buildings around it.

glossary.plain_meaning: Sick holds example interface designs made to look polished.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not required for building interfaces.
glossary.example: A fully designed train control panel shown as inspiration.

glossary.plain_meaning: Basic Widgets are simple interface parts like buttons and sliders.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not full interfaces by themselves.
glossary.example: Basic switches used to start or stop a train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Core holds the most basic and necessary parts.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not optional extras.
glossary.example: The main tracks every train must use.

glossary.plain_meaning: Gadgets are small ready-made parts that do one clear thing.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not large systems.
glossary.example: A small device that checks the train’s speed.

glossary.plain_meaning: Package groups parts that are meant to be used together.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not single standalone pieces.
glossary.example: A full train kit that comes with engine, cars, and controls.

glossary.plain_meaning: Scripting groups parts that use written instructions to control behavior.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not visual-only tools.
glossary.example: Written instructions that tell the train when to stop or go.

glossary.plain_meaning: Tools group parts that help build, manage, or inspect things.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not the final result.
glossary.example: Inspection tools used to check the train system.

glossary.plain_meaning: Window Placement controls how the main window behaves and where it appears.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change the content itself.
glossary.mental_model: Rules for where a window sits.
glossary.example: Deciding whether you are in the control room watching the train system or out on the platform showing it to others.

glossary.plain_meaning: Designer Mode is for building and adjusting things.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not meant for showing a finished result.
glossary.example: Engineers walking around the tracks while the train system is being worked on.

glossary.plain_meaning: Performer Mode is for showing the result without editing.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not for changing how things are built.
glossary.example: Passengers watching the train run without seeing the controls.

glossary.plain_meaning: Par means the window follows settings from a parameter.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not manually positioned.
glossary.example: The train’s position is decided by signals, not by someone pushing it.

glossary.plain_meaning: Net means the window placement is linked to the network.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not an independent window.
glossary.example: The train moves because it is connected to the rail network.

glossary.plain_meaning: Open and Close control whether the window is visible or hidden.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not stop the system from running.
glossary.example: Opening or closing the door so you can see the train pass.

glossary.plain_meaning: Path defines which part of the system the window is connected to.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not the content itself.
glossary.example: Choosing which track you are watching the train on.

glossary.plain_meaning: Custom Position lets you place the window exactly where you want.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not affect how the system works.
glossary.example: Standing in a specific spot on the platform to watch the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: MIDI Mapper is where you connect MIDI controls to things in TouchDesigner.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not create sound or visuals by itself.
glossary.mental_model: A place to hook controls to actions.
glossary.example: A control box that lets you change train signals using a handheld controller.

glossary.plain_meaning: Device Mappings is the list of all MIDI connections you have set up.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not the MIDI data itself.
glossary.example: A list showing which switches control which train signals.

glossary.plain_meaning: In Device is the MIDI device that sends messages into TouchDesigner.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not the device TouchDesigner sends messages to.
glossary.example: A handheld controller sending a signal to change a train light.

glossary.plain_meaning: Out Device is the MIDI device that TouchDesigner sends messages to.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not the device sending messages into TouchDesigner.
glossary.example: TouchDesigner sending a signal out to a train control box.

glossary.plain_meaning: MIDI Map is the set of rules that say which MIDI message controls which thing.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not the MIDI device itself.
glossary.example: A note that says: this knob changes the train speed control.

glossary.plain_meaning: Ch means which MIDI channel a message uses.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not the message content.
glossary.example: One train track out of many tracks, each with its own lane number.

glossary.plain_meaning: Create New Mapping makes a new connection between a MIDI control and something in TouchDesigner.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not delete or change existing mappings.
glossary.example: Adding a new rule so a button can switch a train signal.

glossary.plain_meaning: Check MIDI Devices looks for MIDI devices that are connected and available.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not create devices or fix hardware.
glossary.mental_model: Scan for what is plugged in.
glossary.example: Checking which control boxes are connected to the train system.

glossary.plain_meaning: MIDI Console shows the MIDI messages that are coming in or going out.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change the messages.
glossary.example: A display that shows every signal sent to the train controls.

glossary.plain_meaning: ID is the label that helps tell one message apart from another.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not what the message means.
glossary.example: A tag on a train car that identifies it.

glossary.plain_meaning: Type tells what kind of MIDI message it is.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not the value of the message.
glossary.example: A label saying this train signal is a stop signal, not a speed signal.

glossary.plain_meaning: Channel tells which MIDI channel the message is on.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not which device sent it.
glossary.example: Which track the train used to arrive at the station.

glossary.plain_meaning: Index is the number that points to which control or part the message refers to.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not the strength of the message.
glossary.example: Button number 3 on a control panel.

glossary.plain_meaning: Value is the number carried by the message.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not the message type.
glossary.example: How much the train speed control is being turned up or down.

glossary.plain_meaning: Message is the full MIDI information being shown in the console.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not a human sentence.
glossary.example: A short signal sent to the train system that says what just happened.

glossary.plain_meaning: Console Settings control what the MIDI console shows.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These settings do not change the MIDI data itself.
glossary.mental_model: Filters for what you are allowed to see.
glossary.example: Deciding which train signals are shown on a control screen.

glossary.plain_meaning: Enable Logging turns message recording on or off.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change how MIDI works.
glossary.mental_model: Start or stop writing things down.
glossary.example: Turning on a logbook that records when trains pass.

glossary.plain_meaning: In Messages shows MIDI messages coming into TouchDesigner.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not show messages being sent out.
glossary.example: Seeing signals arrive that control the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Out Messages shows MIDI messages being sent out from TouchDesigner.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not show incoming messages.
glossary.example: Seeing signals sent from the control room to the train system.

glossary.plain_meaning: Clock and Timing Messages show signals related to timing and sync.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not control changes or notes.
glossary.example: Signals that keep multiple trains moving in step.

glossary.plain_meaning: Sensing Messages show signals used to check if devices are still connected.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not actions or commands.
glossary.example: Regular pings that confirm the train control system is still online.

glossary.plain_meaning: Beat Panel is where timing based on rhythm is handled.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not make sound by itself.
glossary.example: Keeping the train moving in time with a steady beat.

glossary.plain_meaning: Time Panel shows timing information related to beats.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not control the timing.
glossary.example: A screen that shows when the train should move in rhythm.

glossary.plain_meaning: Beat Input is where beat information comes from.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not the beat itself.
glossary.example: A signal telling the train when to move based on a beat.

glossary.plain_meaning: Listen and Tap are ways to set a beat by listening or tapping.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not play music.
glossary.example: Tapping your foot to set when the train should move.

glossary.plain_meaning: Tempo is how fast the beat is.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not the beat pattern.
glossary.example: How fast the train moves when following the beat.

glossary.plain_meaning: This area is about names that stand in for values or text so they can be reused.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not visuals or sound.
glossary.mental_model: Short names that mean something else.
glossary.example: Giving a train route a short name so you don’t have to say the full route every time.

glossary.plain_meaning: Environment variables are values set outside the project that the project can read.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not settings saved inside the project.
glossary.example: Weather conditions that affect how the train runs, even though they are set outside the station.

glossary.plain_meaning: Built-in variables are values TouchDesigner already knows and provides automatically.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not custom names you invent.
glossary.example: The train system already knows the current time without being told.

glossary.plain_meaning: Macros are shortcuts that expand into longer text or values.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not commands that run by themselves.
glossary.example: Writing a short code that always expands to the full train route name.

glossary.plain_meaning: The console is a place where messages from the system are shown.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not control or change anything.
glossary.example: A screen that shows notes about what the train system is doing right now.

glossary.plain_meaning: Textport is a place where you type text commands and see text responses.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not a place where visuals or sound are shown.
glossary.example: Typing instructions into a control room terminal to check on the train system.

glossary.plain_meaning: Signal flow taxonomy is the system TouchDesigner uses to describe how information moves and connects.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not a set of rules you must memorize.
glossary.mental_model: A map of how trains are allowed to move.
glossary.example: Rules that explain which tracks trains can use and how they connect.

glossary.plain_meaning: An input is where information enters something.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not where information ends.
glossary.example: Where the train enters the rail system.

glossary.plain_meaning: An output is where information leaves something.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not where information begins.
glossary.example: Where the train leaves one section of track.

glossary.plain_meaning: One-to-one means one thing feeds into one other thing.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not combining multiple sources.
glossary.example: One train going from one track directly to the next.

glossary.plain_meaning: One-to-many means one thing feeds into multiple things.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not multiple things merging together.
glossary.example: One train signal controlling several different tracks.

glossary.plain_meaning: Many-to-one means multiple things feed into one thing.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not keeping sources separate.
glossary.example: Several tracks joining into one main track.

glossary.plain_meaning: A chain is a straight line of things connected one after another.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not branching or merging.
glossary.example: The train moving through stations one by one in order.

glossary.plain_meaning: A branch is where one output feeds into multiple paths.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not a merge.
glossary.example: One train signal sending instructions to two different routes.

glossary.plain_meaning: A merge is where multiple inputs come together into one.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not keeping inputs separate.
glossary.example: Two trains entering the same main line.

glossary.plain_meaning: Pass-through means information goes through unchanged.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not transforming the information.
glossary.example: A train passing through a station without stopping.

glossary.plain_meaning: Process means information is changed as it moves through.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not simple forwarding.
glossary.example: The train changes speed while moving through a section of track.

glossary.plain_meaning: Cook dependency means one thing updates because something before it changed.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not random updating.
glossary.example: The train moves because the signal before it changed.

glossary.plain_meaning: Upstream means earlier in the flow.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not what comes after.
glossary.example: The part of the track the train already passed.

glossary.plain_meaning: Downstream means later in the flow.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not what comes before.
glossary.mental_model: Ahead of you on the track.
glossary.example: The part of the track the train is about to reach.

glossary.plain_meaning: Family-specific flow rules describe which kinds of data are allowed to connect to each other.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not about how to connect things.
glossary.mental_model: Rules about which tracks fit which trains.
glossary.example: A freight train can use cargo tracks, but not passenger-only tracks.

glossary.plain_meaning: Image data is information that represents pictures.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not text or numbers.
glossary.example: The video of the train moving along the tracks.

glossary.plain_meaning: Value data is information made of changing numbers.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not an image.
glossary.example: The speed of the train going up or down.

glossary.plain_meaning: Shape data describes form and structure in space.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not a flat picture.
glossary.example: The shape of the train and the rails.

glossary.plain_meaning: Text data is information written as words, lists, or tables.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not visual imagery.
glossary.example: A written schedule of train stops.

glossary.plain_meaning: Surface data describes how something looks on the outside.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not define shape.
glossary.example: The paint and finish on the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Compatible means two things can connect directly.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not mean they will work well together.
glossary.example: Two rail sections that fit together.

glossary.plain_meaning: Incompatible means two things cannot connect directly.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not mean either thing is broken.
glossary.example: Trying to run a train on the wrong type of track.

glossary.plain_meaning: Connection words are the terms TouchDesigner uses to describe how data is shared.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not instructions.
glossary.example: Signs that explain how trains share tracks.

glossary.plain_meaning: Connect means linking an output to an input.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change the data.
glossary.example: Linking one section of track to the next.

glossary.plain_meaning: Reference means pointing to something without copying it.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not a duplicate.
glossary.example: A sign that points to the train instead of moving the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Export means sending information out to be used elsewhere.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not keep control inside.
glossary.example: Sending train timing information to another control room.

glossary.plain_meaning: Link means keeping two things connected so they stay in sync.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not a one-time copy.
glossary.example: Two signals that always change together on the train line.

glossary.plain_meaning: Bind means locking one thing to follow another.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not a loose connection.
glossary.example: A train car that must follow the engine.

glossary.plain_meaning: Fetch means pulling information from somewhere else.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not push information out.
glossary.example: Checking another station to see where the train is.

glossary.plain_meaning: Pull means something updates because it is asked for. Push means something updates because it decides to send data out.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not about speed or performance.
glossary.example: A train moves because the next station asks for it to arrive, not because it moves on its own.

glossary.plain_meaning: Demand means something only updates when something else needs it.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not constant updating.
glossary.example: The train only moves when a station expects it, not when no one is watching.

glossary.plain_meaning: Active means something is currently being used. Inactive means it is not being used.
glossary.what_it_is_not: Inactive does not mean deleted or broken.
glossary.example: A train on the main track is active. A train parked on a side track is inactive.

glossary.plain_meaning: Order of evaluation is the order in which things are checked and updated.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not random.
glossary.example: Signals are checked before the train is allowed to move forward.

glossary.plain_meaning: Side effects are changes that happen beyond the main result.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not the main output.
glossary.example: Changing a train signal also affects nearby tracks, even if that wasn’t the main goal.

glossary.plain_meaning: Ownership means which part is responsible for the data.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not shared responsibility.
glossary.example: One station is responsible for scheduling the train, not every station.

glossary.plain_meaning: Scope means how far something can be seen or used.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not about physical distance.
glossary.example: Some train signals affect the whole rail system, others only affect one station.

glossary.plain_meaning: Operator families are groups that tell you what kind of data an operator works with.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not about what an operator looks like or how hard it is to use.
glossary.mental_model: Different types of train cars for different cargo.
glossary.example: Some train cars carry people, some carry boxes, some carry fuel.

glossary.plain_meaning: The TOP family works with images.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These do not work with numbers or text.
glossary.example: The video of a train moving along the tracks.

glossary.plain_meaning: The CHOP family works with changing numbers over time.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not images.
glossary.example: The train’s speed going up and down as it moves.

glossary.plain_meaning: The SOP family works with shapes and structure in space.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not flat pictures.
glossary.example: The shape of the train and the rails.

glossary.plain_meaning: The DAT family works with text, tables, and lists.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These do not directly show images or motion.
glossary.example: A written schedule showing when the train stops.

glossary.plain_meaning: The MAT family works with how surfaces look.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These do not define shape or movement.
glossary.example: The color and finish painted on the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Family compatibility means some families can connect directly and others cannot.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not mean incompatible families are wrong or useless.
glossary.mental_model: Matching cargo to the right train car.
glossary.example: You don’t put liquid fuel in a passenger car.

glossary.plain_meaning: Family boundaries explain where data must change form to keep moving.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not an error or failure.
glossary.example: Moving cargo from one type of train car into another.

glossary.plain_meaning: Shared Mem In receives data from another program using shared memory.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not send data out or create data.
glossary.example: Another train system leaves cargo on a shared platform, and this train picks it up.

glossary.plain_meaning: Setup contains the settings needed to identify and connect to shared memory.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change the data itself.
glossary.example: Naming which platform the train should stop at.

glossary.plain_meaning: Shared Mem Name is the name used to find the shared memory.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not the data inside the memory.
glossary.example: The name painted on the platform where cargo is shared.

glossary.plain_meaning: objsharedmem is the default shared memory name used by TouchDesigner.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not special or required.
glossary.example: A standard platform name used unless changed.

glossary.plain_meaning: Shared Mem Type describes what kind of data is being received.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not describe where the data comes from.
glossary.example: Knowing whether the train is picking up boxes or liquid.

glossary.plain_meaning: Extensions holds extra behaviors added to the component.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not required for basic operation.
glossary.example: Extra equipment added to the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Promote Extension makes extension features visible at a higher level.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change how the component works.
glossary.example: Moving train controls closer to the driver.

glossary.plain_meaning: Re-Init Extension resets the extension logic.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not reset the whole component.
glossary.example: Resetting the train’s onboard computer without stopping the engine.

glossary.plain_meaning: Common contains settings shared by many components.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These settings are not unique to shared memory.
glossary.example: Rules that apply to many stations.

glossary.plain_meaning: Viewer Active controls whether the viewer is updating.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not stop the component itself.
glossary.example: Turning the station monitor on or off.

glossary.plain_meaning: Cook Type controls when the component updates.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not control what the component does.
glossary.example: Deciding when the train should move.

glossary.plain_meaning: Node View controls how the component appears in the network.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not affect behavior.
glossary.mental_model: How it looks on the map.
glossary.example: How a station icon looks on the rail map.

glossary.plain_meaning: Clone allows the component to follow another one.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not an independent copy.
glossary.example: One train copying the behavior of another.

glossary.plain_meaning: Shared Mem Out sends data from TouchDesigner to another program using shared memory.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not receive data from other programs.
glossary.example: Your train places cargo onto a shared platform so another train system can pick it up.

glossary.plain_meaning: Setup contains the settings needed to send data to shared memory.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change the data itself.
glossary.example: Choosing which platform the train will leave cargo on.

glossary.plain_meaning: Active controls whether data is being sent out or not.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not delete or disconnect anything.
glossary.example: Deciding whether the train is currently dropping off cargo.

glossary.plain_meaning: Shared Mem Name is the name used so other programs know where to find the shared data.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not the data itself.
glossary.example: The name written on the shared platform.

glossary.plain_meaning: objsharedmem is the default shared memory name used by TouchDesigner.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not required or special.
glossary.example: A standard platform name used unless changed.

glossary.plain_meaning: Shared Mem Type describes what kind of data is being sent.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not describe who receives it.
glossary.example: Whether the train is sending boxes, liquid, or signals.

glossary.plain_meaning: Extensions holds extra behavior added to the component.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not required for basic sending.
glossary.example: Extra equipment added to the train for special situations.

glossary.plain_meaning: Promote Extension makes extension features visible at a higher level.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change how data is sent.
glossary.example: Moving train controls closer to the operator.

glossary.plain_meaning: Re-Init Extension restarts the extension logic.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not restart the whole component.
glossary.example: Resetting the train’s onboard helper computer.

glossary.plain_meaning: Common contains settings shared by many components.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These settings are not specific to shared memory.
glossary.example: Rules that apply to many stations.

glossary.plain_meaning: Viewer Active controls whether the viewer is updating.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not stop data from being sent.
glossary.example: Turning the station monitor on or off.

glossary.plain_meaning: Cook Type controls when the component updates.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change what is being sent.
glossary.example: Deciding when the train should drop off cargo.

glossary.plain_meaning: Node View controls how the component appears in the network.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not affect behavior.
glossary.example: How the station icon looks on the rail map.

glossary.plain_meaning: Clone allows the component to follow another one.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not an independent copy.
glossary.example: One train copying another train’s behavior.

glossary.plain_meaning: Ambient Light adds overall light that affects everything evenly.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not a directional or focused light.
glossary.example: Soft daylight that lights the whole train yard at once.

glossary.plain_meaning: Light contains settings that control the basic color and strength of the light.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not control how objects are drawn.
glossary.example: Deciding what color the daylight is around the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Light Color sets the color of the ambient light.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change object colors directly.
glossary.example: Making the light around the train warm or cool.

glossary.plain_meaning: Dimmer controls how strong the ambient light is.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not turn the light into a spotlight.
glossary.example: Turning the overall daylight brighter or darker in the train yard.

glossary.plain_meaning: Render contains settings that affect how things are drawn.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change the light itself.
glossary.example: Rules for how the train and tracks are drawn on screen.

glossary.plain_meaning: Material Render controls whether materials are used when drawing.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change object shape.
glossary.example: Deciding whether the train uses its paint and finish.

glossary.plain_meaning: Draw Priority controls which things are drawn first or last.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move objects in space.
glossary.example: Deciding whether the train is drawn before or after the background.

glossary.plain_meaning: Pick Priority controls which object is chosen when things overlap.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not affect drawing order.
glossary.example: Choosing which train car gets selected when two overlap.

glossary.plain_meaning: Wireframe Color sets the color used for wireframe display.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not affect the final look.
glossary.example: Changing the color of the outline drawn around the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Extensions holds extra behavior added to the component.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not required for basic use.
glossary.example: Extra systems added to the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Extension refers to added logic attached to the component.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not the main function.
glossary.example: A helper computer added to the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Object refers to the component itself as a controllable item.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not the data it produces.
glossary.example: The train, not the cargo it carries.

glossary.plain_meaning: Re-Init Extensions resets the extension logic.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not reset the whole component.
glossary.example: Restarting the train’s helper systems.

glossary.plain_meaning: Init Extensions on Start controls whether extensions start automatically.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not start the main component.
glossary.example: Helper systems turning on when the train starts.

glossary.plain_meaning: Common contains settings shared by many components.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not unique to lighting.
glossary.example: Rules shared by many train stations.

glossary.plain_meaning: Parent Shortcut is a quick way to refer to the parent component.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not a copy.
glossary.example: A quick route back to the main station.

glossary.plain_meaning: Global OP Shortcut is a name used to access this component globally.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not local only.
glossary.example: A train station name known across the whole system.

glossary.plain_meaning: Internal OP Shortcut is a name used inside the component.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not visible everywhere.
glossary.example: A nickname used only inside one station.

glossary.plain_meaning: Node View controls how the component looks in the network.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not affect behavior.
glossary.example: How the station icon looks on the rail map.

glossary.plain_meaning: Enable Cloning allows the component to follow another one.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not make an independent copy.
glossary.example: One train copying another train’s actions.

glossary.plain_meaning: Clone Master is the component being followed.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not a separate copy.
glossary.example: The lead train others follow.

glossary.plain_meaning: Load on Demand means parts load only when needed.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not always loaded.
glossary.example: A train that only arrives when requested.

glossary.plain_meaning: Enable External TOX allows the component to load from a file.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not an internal component.
glossary.example: Bringing in a train car built elsewhere.

glossary.plain_meaning: External TOX Path is where the external file is located.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not the file itself.
glossary.example: The address of the train factory.

glossary.plain_meaning: Reload Custom Parameters refreshes custom settings.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not reload everything.
glossary.example: Updating custom train controls.

glossary.plain_meaning: Reload Built-In Parameters refreshes default settings.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not reload custom settings.
glossary.example: Refreshing standard train controls.

glossary.plain_meaning: Sub-Component to Load selects which part to load from a file.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not the whole file.
glossary.example: Selecting one train car from a full train set.

glossary.plain_meaning: Relative File Path Behavior controls how file paths are handled.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change the file.
glossary.example: Deciding how directions to the train depot are interpreted.

glossary.plain_meaning: Blend combines multiple things into one result.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not create new content by itself.
glossary.example: Two train routes merging into one shared track.

glossary.plain_meaning: Blend Type decides how things are combined.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not choose what is being combined.
glossary.example: Deciding whether two trains take turns or overlap.

glossary.plain_meaning: Sequence controls the order things are combined.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change the content itself.
glossary.example: Which train enters the track first.

glossary.plain_meaning: Reset Frame resets timing used by the blend.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not reset the whole system.
glossary.example: Sending the train back to the start of the schedule.

glossary.plain_meaning: Weight controls how much influence something has.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not turn things on or off.
glossary.example: One train having more priority on the track.

glossary.plain_meaning: Mask limits where blending happens.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change the blended content.
glossary.example: Only blending trains on certain sections of track.

glossary.plain_meaning: Normal Offset shifts blending along a surface direction.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not rotate objects.
glossary.example: Sliding the train slightly sideways on the track.

glossary.plain_meaning: Orient controls how direction is aligned.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move position.
glossary.example: Which way the train is pointing.

glossary.plain_meaning: Axes define directions used for orientation.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not positions.
glossary.example: Forward, left, and up directions for the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Short Rotation chooses the shortest turn direction.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change final orientation.
glossary.example: The train turning the quickest way to face forward.

glossary.plain_meaning: Xform controls position, rotation, and size.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change appearance or color.
glossary.example: Moving and rotating the train on the tracks.

glossary.plain_meaning: Transform Order controls the order of movement steps.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change the values themselves.
glossary.example: Turning the train before moving it.

glossary.plain_meaning: Rotate Order controls how rotations are applied.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not set rotation amount.
glossary.example: Which way the train turns first.

glossary.plain_meaning: Translate moves position.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not rotate or resize.
glossary.example: The train moving forward.

glossary.plain_meaning: Rotate turns orientation.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move position.
glossary.example: The train turning a corner.

glossary.plain_meaning: Scale changes size.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move or rotate.
glossary.example: The train appearing bigger or smaller.

glossary.plain_meaning: Pivot sets the point rotations happen around.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move the object.
glossary.example: The axle the train turns around.

glossary.plain_meaning: Uniform Scale keeps size changes equal in all directions.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not control position.
glossary.example: The train growing evenly.

glossary.plain_meaning: Parent Transform Source chooses where inherited movement comes from.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not manual movement.
glossary.example: A train car following the engine.

glossary.plain_meaning: Look At makes something face a target.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move position.
glossary.example: The train always facing the next station.

glossary.plain_meaning: Path OP defines a path to follow.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not define speed.
glossary.example: The track the train follows.

glossary.plain_meaning: Orient Along Path makes orientation follow the path direction.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change the path itself.
glossary.example: The train turning as the track curves.

glossary.plain_meaning: Pre-Xform applies movement before the main transform.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not the final transform.
glossary.example: Adjusting the train before placing it on the track.

glossary.plain_meaning: Apply Pre-Transform turns pre-transform on or off.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change main transform values.
glossary.example: Deciding whether the early adjustment is used.

glossary.plain_meaning: Reset Transform clears transform values.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not delete the object.
glossary.example: Putting the train back to the starting position.

glossary.plain_meaning: Commit to Main Transform moves pre-transform into the main transform.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not add a second transform.
glossary.example: Locking in the train’s adjusted position.

glossary.plain_meaning: Render controls how things are drawn.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move or change data.
glossary.example: Rules for how the train appears on screen.

glossary.plain_meaning: Material defines surface appearance.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not define shape.
glossary.example: The paint on the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Light Mask controls which lights affect this object.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change the lights themselves.
glossary.example: Only certain lights shining on the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Extensions hold extra behavior logic.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not required for basic use.
glossary.example: Optional systems added to the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Common contains shared component settings.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not unique to blending.
glossary.example: Rules shared by many stations.

glossary.plain_meaning: Blend combines multiple things into one result.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not create new content by itself.
glossary.example: Two train routes merging into one shared track.

glossary.plain_meaning: Blend Type decides how things are combined.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not choose what is being combined.
glossary.example: Deciding whether two trains take turns or overlap.

glossary.plain_meaning: Sequence controls the order things are combined.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change the content itself.
glossary.example: Which train enters the track first.

glossary.plain_meaning: Reset Frame resets timing used by the blend.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not reset the whole system.
glossary.example: Sending the train back to the start of the schedule.

glossary.plain_meaning: Weight controls how much influence something has.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not turn things on or off.
glossary.example: One train having more priority on the track.

glossary.plain_meaning: Mask limits where blending happens.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change the blended content.
glossary.example: Only blending trains on certain sections of track.

glossary.plain_meaning: Normal Offset shifts blending along a surface direction.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not rotate objects.
glossary.example: Sliding the train slightly sideways on the track.

glossary.plain_meaning: Orient controls how direction is aligned.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move position.
glossary.example: Which way the train is pointing.

glossary.plain_meaning: Axes define directions used for orientation.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not positions.
glossary.example: Forward, left, and up directions for the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Short Rotation chooses the shortest turn direction.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change final orientation.
glossary.example: The train turning the quickest way to face forward.

glossary.plain_meaning: Xform controls position, rotation, and size.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change appearance or color.
glossary.example: Moving and rotating the train on the tracks.

glossary.plain_meaning: Transform Order controls the order of movement steps.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change the values themselves.
glossary.example: Turning the train before moving it.

glossary.plain_meaning: Rotate Order controls how rotations are applied.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not set rotation amount.
glossary.example: Which way the train turns first.

glossary.plain_meaning: Translate moves position.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not rotate or resize.
glossary.example: The train moving forward.

glossary.plain_meaning: Rotate turns orientation.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move position.
glossary.example: The train turning a corner.

glossary.plain_meaning: Scale changes size.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move or rotate.
glossary.example: The train appearing bigger or smaller.

glossary.plain_meaning: Pivot sets the point rotations happen around.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move the object.
glossary.example: The axle the train turns around.

glossary.plain_meaning: Uniform Scale keeps size changes equal in all directions.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not control position.
glossary.example: The train growing evenly.

glossary.plain_meaning: Parent Transform Source chooses where inherited movement comes from.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not manual movement.
glossary.example: A train car following the engine.

glossary.plain_meaning: Look At makes something face a target.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move position.
glossary.example: The train always facing the next station.

glossary.plain_meaning: Path OP defines a path to follow.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not define speed.
glossary.example: The track the train follows.

glossary.plain_meaning: Orient Along Path makes orientation follow the path direction.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change the path itself.
glossary.example: The train turning as the track curves.

glossary.plain_meaning: Pre-Xform applies movement before the main transform.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not the final transform.
glossary.example: Adjusting the train before placing it on the track.

glossary.plain_meaning: Apply Pre-Transform turns pre-transform on or off.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change main transform values.
glossary.example: Deciding whether the early adjustment is used.

glossary.plain_meaning: Reset Transform clears transform values.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not delete the object.
glossary.example: Putting the train back to the starting position.

glossary.plain_meaning: Commit to Main Transform moves pre-transform into the main transform.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not add a second transform.
glossary.example: Locking in the train’s adjusted position.

glossary.plain_meaning: Render controls how things are drawn.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move or change data.
glossary.example: Rules for how the train appears on screen.

glossary.plain_meaning: Material defines surface appearance.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not define shape.
glossary.example: The paint on the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Light Mask controls which lights affect this object.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change the lights themselves.
glossary.example: Only certain lights shining on the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Extensions hold extra behavior logic.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not required for basic use.
glossary.example: Optional systems added to the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Common contains shared component settings.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not unique to blending.
glossary.example: Rules shared by many stations.

glossary.plain_meaning: A Bone controls movement and bending for connected parts, usually in a chain.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not a visible object by itself.
glossary.example: Each car of a train connected so the whole train can bend along the track.

glossary.plain_meaning: Xform controls position, rotation, and size.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not control bending behavior.
glossary.example: Placing the train on the tracks and pointing it forward.

glossary.plain_meaning: Transform Order sets the order movement steps happen.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change movement values.
glossary.example: Turning the train before moving it.

glossary.plain_meaning: Rotate Order sets the order rotations are applied.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not set how much rotation happens.
glossary.example: Which way the train turns first.

glossary.plain_meaning: Translate moves position.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not rotate or resize.
glossary.example: The train moving forward.

glossary.plain_meaning: Rotate turns direction.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move position.
glossary.example: The train turning a corner.

glossary.plain_meaning: Scale changes size.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move position.
glossary.example: The train appearing larger.

glossary.plain_meaning: Pivot sets the point rotation happens around.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move the object.
glossary.example: The axle the train turns around.

glossary.plain_meaning: Uniform Scale keeps size changes equal in all directions.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not control position.
glossary.example: The train growing evenly.

glossary.plain_meaning: Parent Transform Source decides what movement this follows.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not manual movement.
glossary.example: A train car following the engine.

glossary.plain_meaning: Look At makes the bone face a target.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move position.
glossary.example: The train always facing the next station.

glossary.plain_meaning: Forward Direction defines what direction counts as forward.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move the bone.
glossary.example: Defining the front of the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Up Vector defines which direction is considered up.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not rotate by itself.
glossary.example: Keeping the train upright.

glossary.plain_meaning: Path OP defines a path to follow.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not define speed.
glossary.example: The tracks the train follows.

glossary.plain_meaning: Roll Position controls rotation along the path.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move along the path.
glossary.example: The train tilting slightly on a curve.

glossary.plain_meaning: Orient Along Path makes direction follow the path.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change the path.
glossary.example: The train turning as the track curves.

glossary.plain_meaning: Orient Up Vector keeps orientation aligned upright.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not control movement.
glossary.example: The train staying upright on slopes.

glossary.plain_meaning: Auto Bank Factor controls how much automatic tilt happens on turns.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move forward.
glossary.example: The train leaning into curves.

glossary.plain_meaning: Pre-Xform applies movement before the main transform.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not the final position.
glossary.example: Adjusting the train before placing it.

glossary.plain_meaning: Apply Pre-Transform turns the pre-transform on or off.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change main transform values.
glossary.example: Choosing whether early adjustments count.

glossary.plain_meaning: Reset Transform clears transform values.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not delete the bone.
glossary.example: Putting the train back to start.

glossary.plain_meaning: Commit to Main Transform makes the pre-transform permanent.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not add another transform.
glossary.example: Fixing the train’s position permanently.

glossary.plain_meaning: Bone settings control bending and limits.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These do not move the whole chain.
glossary.example: Rules for how train cars can bend.

glossary.plain_meaning: Display Link shows the bone connection.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not affect behavior.
glossary.example: Seeing the connection between train cars.

glossary.plain_meaning: Rest Angles define the default angle.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not movement.
glossary.example: The train when not turning.

glossary.plain_meaning: Bone Length controls how long the bone is.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not control rotation.
glossary.example: Distance between train joints.

glossary.plain_meaning: Dampening softens movement.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not stop movement.
glossary.example: The train not snapping suddenly.

glossary.plain_meaning: Capture controls how geometry is influenced by bones.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move bones.
glossary.example: Which train cars are affected by a joint.

glossary.plain_meaning: Display Capture Geometry shows the affected area.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change behavior.
glossary.example: Seeing which cars are affected by a joint.

glossary.plain_meaning: Render controls how things are drawn.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change movement.
glossary.example: How the train is drawn on screen.

glossary.plain_meaning: Extensions hold extra behavior logic.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not required.
glossary.example: Extra systems on the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Common contains shared component settings.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not bone-specific.
glossary.example: Rules shared by many stations.

glossary.plain_meaning: Camera defines how the scene is viewed.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not create objects or light.
glossary.example: Where the train yard is being viewed from.

glossary.plain_meaning: Xform controls where the camera is, how it is turned, and its size scale.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not control what the camera sees.
glossary.example: Walking around the train yard and turning your head.

glossary.plain_meaning: Transform Order sets the order that movement steps happen.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change the values themselves.
glossary.example: Turning before stepping forward.

glossary.plain_meaning: Rotate Order sets the order rotations are applied.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change how much rotation there is.
glossary.example: Looking left before looking up.

glossary.plain_meaning: Translate moves the camera.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not rotate the camera.
glossary.example: Walking closer to the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Rotate turns the camera.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move the camera.
glossary.example: Looking toward a different track.

glossary.plain_meaning: Scale changes the camera’s scale space.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not zoom.
glossary.example: Changing how large the world feels.

glossary.plain_meaning: Pivot sets the point rotations happen around.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move the camera by itself.
glossary.example: Turning your head around your neck.

glossary.plain_meaning: Uniform Scale keeps scale equal in all directions.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not affect position.
glossary.example: Everything shrinking evenly.

glossary.plain_meaning: Parent Transform Source makes the camera follow another object.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not manual control.
glossary.example: The camera riding on a train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Look At makes the camera face a target.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move the camera.
glossary.example: The camera always looking at the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Look At Up Vector defines what direction is considered up while looking.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change the target.
glossary.example: Keeping the camera from flipping upside down.

glossary.plain_meaning: Path OP defines a path the camera can follow.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not define speed.
glossary.example: A rail the camera moves along beside the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Pre-Xform applies movement before the main transform.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This is not the final position.
glossary.example: Adjusting your stance before looking.

glossary.plain_meaning: Apply Pre-Transform turns the pre-transform on or off.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change main transform values.
glossary.example: Deciding whether the early adjustment matters.

glossary.plain_meaning: Reset Transform clears transform values.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not delete the camera.
glossary.example: Putting the camera back to default.

glossary.plain_meaning: Commit to Main Transform makes the pre-transform permanent.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not add another transform.
glossary.example: Fixing the camera position permanently.

glossary.plain_meaning: View controls how the camera sees the world.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move the camera.
glossary.example: Choosing how wide the train yard looks.

glossary.plain_meaning: Projection controls how 3D space is flattened into an image.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move objects.
glossary.example: Wide-angle versus flat view of the train yard.

glossary.plain_meaning: Viewing Angle Method chooses how viewing angles are calculated.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change camera position.
glossary.example: How wide the camera sees.

glossary.plain_meaning: FOV Angle controls how wide the camera sees.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move the camera.
glossary.example: Seeing the whole train yard or just one track.

glossary.plain_meaning: Near sets how close objects can be before disappearing.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not affect far objects.
glossary.example: The train disappearing when too close.

glossary.plain_meaning: Far sets how distant objects can be before disappearing.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not affect near objects.
glossary.example: The train fading out in the distance.

glossary.plain_meaning: Window Roll rotates the camera view.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move the camera.
glossary.example: Tilting your head while watching the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: IPD Shift adjusts eye separation for stereo viewing.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not affect mono view.
glossary.example: Seeing depth like human eyes do.

glossary.plain_meaning: Settings control background and rendering behavior.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change camera position.
glossary.example: Deciding what the sky behind the train looks like.

glossary.plain_meaning: Background Color sets the color behind everything.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not affect objects.
glossary.example: The sky color behind the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Fog fades distant objects.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not move objects.
glossary.example: The train fading into mist.

glossary.plain_meaning: Camera Light Mask controls which lights affect the camera view.
glossary.what_it_is_not: This does not change the lights themselves.
glossary.example: Only certain lights visible on the train.

glossary.plain_meaning: Extensions add extra behavior logic.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not required.
glossary.example: Extra camera control systems.

glossary.plain_meaning: Common contains shared component settings.
glossary.what_it_is_not: These are not camera-specific.
glossary.example: Rules shared by many stations.
